message.content: {"schema_version":"stella.literature_hvs_candidates.v0.1","generated_at":"2026-06-13T09:21:32","paper":{"arxiv_id":"2011.10206","bibcode":"2021ApJS..252....3L","title":"591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2","month":"2020-11","source_note_json":"notes/2020/2020-11/2020-11.json","links":{"abs":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2011.10206","pdf":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2011.10206"}},"inputs":{"paper_dir":"literature/2011.10206","audit_path":"literature/2011.10206/audit.json","catalog_review_path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_review.json","catalog_extraction_path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_extraction.json","ecsv_paths":["literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-002.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-003.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-004.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-005.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-006.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-007.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-008.ecsv","literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-009.ecsv"]},"extraction":{"status":"partial","extracted_at":"2026-06-13T09:21:32","extractor":"agent","summary":"Extraction covers only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs (Hivel1–Hivel20) for which individual unbound probabilities and full data are provided in the printed tables (Tables 1, 2, 6, 7, 8). The remaining 571 HiVelSCs from the full 591-object catalog are documented in candidate_groups_considered because the external CSV catalog is not among the provided source files and individual unbound status cannot be extracted from the paper alone.","tooling":{"agent_runtime":"","model_id":"","prompt_version":"","request_parameters":{}}},"method_chain":[{"id":"step-01","depends_on":[],"step_type":"other","summary":"Overview of the two main input data sources used in this work: LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectroscopic survey (10,608,416 spectra) and Gaia DR2 astrometry, photometry, and covariance matrices.","inputs":["LAMOST DR7","Gaia DR2"],"outputs":[],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":180,"end_line":204,"context":"Section 2.1 description of LAMOST and Gaia"}]},{"id":"step-02","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"cross_match","summary":"Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using a 5 arcsec radius via TOPCAT, producing the LAMOST-Gaia catalog of over 10 million entries.","inputs":["LAMOST DR7","Gaia DR2"],"outputs":["LAMOST-Gaia cross-matched catalog"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":270,"end_line":272,"context":"Cross-match description with TOPCAT"}]},{"id":"step-03","depends_on":["step-02"],"step_type":"sample_selection","summary":"Select HiVelSC sample: require positive parallax, fractional parallax error f_varpi <= 0.2; for multi-epoch spectra keep highest S/N; visually inspect RV consistency. Resulting in 591 candidates. Subsequently apply zero-point corrected LASP RV (step-04) and recalculate V_GC.","inputs":["LAMOST-Gaia catalog"],"outputs":["591 HiVelSCs"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":254,"end_line":280,"context":"Sample selection and RV checks in Section 2.3"}]},{"id":"step-04","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"radial_velocity_measurement","summary":"Adopt LASP RV for all HiVelSCs because LASP errors include observation/processing uncertainties. Apply zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s determined from cross-match with Huang+2018 RV standard stars.","inputs":["LAMOST LASP RVs","Huang+2018 standard star catalog"],"outputs":["Zero-point corrected RVs for 591 HiVelSCs"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":280,"end_line":288,"context":"RV zero-point correction procedure"}]},{"id":"step-05","depends_on":[],"step_type":"solar_position_and_motion","summary":"Adopted solar position and motion: distance to GC R0 = 8.2 kpc, Sun offset above disk z0 = 25 pc, circular velocity of LSR 238 km/s, solar peculiar velocity [U, V, W] = [14.0, 12.24, 7.25] km/s.","inputs":[],"outputs":["Solar parameters for Galactocentric transformations"],"parameters":[{"name":"R0","raw_value":"8.2","value":"8.2","unit":"kpc","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":210,"end_line":214,"context":"Solar distance and LSR motion"}]},{"name":"z0","raw_value":"25","value":"25","unit":"pc","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":210,"end_line":214,"context":"Sun offset above the disk"}]},{"name":"v_circ_sun","raw_value":"238","value":"238","unit":"km s-1","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":210,"end_line":214,"context":"circular velocity of component"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":210,"end_line":214,"context":"Assumptions for distance and velocity determination"}]},{"id":"step-06","depends_on":["step-03","step-05"],"step_type":"distance_estimation","summary":"Compute heliocentric distance by inverting Gaia parallax (d = 1/varpi). Then compute Galactocentric Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z) and distance r_GC using the solar position from step-05. Monte Carlo 1000 realizations with the full Gaia covariance matrix and Gaussian RV uncertainty; adopt median and 16th/84th percentiles.","inputs":["Gaia DR2 astrometry (parallax, positions, proper motions, covariance)","Solar position (R0, z0)"],"outputs":["r_GC, Galactocentric positions (x,y,z)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":206,"end_line":250,"context":"Distance and total velocity determination (Section 2.2)"}]},{"id":"step-07","depends_on":["step-03","step-04","step-05"],"step_type":"velocity_calculation","summary":"Calculate Galactocentric total velocity V_GC and Cartesian velocity components (Vx, Vy, Vz) from proper motions, corrected RV, and solar motion using the Monte Carlo approach. The Galactic Cartesian coordinate system: X-axis from Sun to GC, Y in direction of Galactic rotation, Z towards North Galactic Pole.","inputs":["Gaia proper motions","Corrected LASP RVs","Solar motion (step-05)"],"outputs":["V_GC, Vx, Vy, Vz"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":206,"end_line":250,"context":"Velocity calculation section"}]},{"id":"step-08","depends_on":[],"step_type":"galactic_potential_model","summary":"Adopt seven Galactic potential models: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014 (Bovy 2015), Xue+2008. These are used to compute escape velocities at each star's position.","inputs":[],"outputs":["Escape velocity functions"],"parameters":[{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Watkins+2019","value":"Watkins+2019","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":274,"end_line":276,"context":"list of seven potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Xue+2008","value":"Xue+2008","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":274,"end_line":276,"context":"list used"}]},{"id":"step-09","depends_on":["step-06","step-08"],"step_type":"escape_or_bound_assessment","summary":"Compute escape velocity V_esc at each star's Galactocentric position for each of the seven potential models, using the potential functions and r_GC from step-06. This step only produces the escape velocity values, without computing unbound probabilities (see step-17).","inputs":["r_GC (step-06)","Potential models (step-08)"],"outputs":["V_esc (per model)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":478,"end_line":480,"context":"escape velocities listed in Table 6"}]},{"id":"step-10","depends_on":["step-06","step-07","step-08"],"step_type":"orbit_integration","summary":"Perform numerical orbit integration backwards in time using the Galpy package with the MWPotential2014 model. For each star, 1000 MC realizations, trace back 10 Gyr with 10 Myr time step. Compute orbital parameters: eccentricity e, maximum height Zmax, minimum disk crossing radius r_min, and energy difference E-Phi(infinity).","inputs":["Galactocentric positions and velocities","MWPotential2014"],"outputs":["e, Zmax, r_min, E-Phi(infinity)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":653,"end_line":667,"context":"Orbit integration with Galpy"}]},{"id":"step-11","depends_on":["step-10"],"step_type":"candidate_classification","summary":"Classify HiVelSCs into four origin types from the computed probabilities: P_gc and P_MW derived from r_min (step-10) and P_ub from step-17 (the classification is described after step-17, but this step captures the classification criteria). Apply thresholds from Table 4: HVS (P_gc > 0.16), HRS (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub > 0.5), RS (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub < 0.5), OUT (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW < 0.5).","inputs":["P_gc","P_MW","P_ub (per model)"],"outputs":["Origin classification (HVS, HRS, RS, OUT)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":704,"end_line":722,"context":"Classification criteria (Section 4, Table 4)"}]},{"id":"step-12","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"stellar_parameter_inference","summary":"Determine atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) and alpha-element abundance [alpha/Fe] using two independent pipelines: LASP (LAMOST Stellar Parameter Pipeline) and DD-Payne (data-driven Payne). Values for the 20 fastest are listed in Table A3.","inputs":["LAMOST spectra for HiVelSCs"],"outputs":["Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H], [alpha/Fe])"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":595,"end_line":599,"context":"DD-Payne alpha-abundance determination"}]},{"id":"step-13","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"quality_filter","summary":"Quality flag 'astrometric_flag' defined to mark stars with conservative high-quality Gaia astrometry, using criteria from Lindegren+2018 (Appendix C) and Marchetti+2019 (Section 4). 476 HiVelSCs satisfy this flag.","inputs":["Gaia astrometric parameters for HiVelSCs"],"outputs":["astrometric_flag (0 or 1)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":516,"end_line":521,"context":"astrometric_flag quality criteria"}]},{"id":"step-14","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"Gaia DR2 astrometry and photometry: positions, proper motions, parallaxes, G/BP/RP magnitudes, and covariance matrix. Provides the fundamental astrometric and photometric measurements used throughout the work.","inputs":["Gaia DR2 catalog"],"outputs":["Gaia DR2 parameters"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":196,"end_line":204,"context":"Gaia DR2 description"}]},{"id":"step-15","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectroscopy: radial velocities (LASP) and stellar atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]), plus spectral classification and S/N_r.","inputs":["LAMOST DR7 catalog"],"outputs":["LAMOST DR7 parameters"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":180,"end_line":194,"context":"LAMOST DR7 description"}]},{"id":"step-16","depends_on":["step-10"],"step_type":"origin_assessment","summary":"Derive origin probabilities from the orbital integration of step-10: P_gc is the fraction of MC orbits with r_min < 1 kpc, P_MW the fraction with r_min < 25 kpc. These probabilities are used in candidate classification.","inputs":["r_min (from step-10)"],"outputs":["P_gc, P_MW"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":702,"end_line":705,"context":"Definition of P_gc and P_MW"}]},{"id":"step-17","depends_on":["step-07","step-09"],"step_type":"escape_or_bound_assessment","summary":"Compute unbound probability P_ub as the fraction of 1000 MC realizations where V_GC > V_esc for each potential model, using the V_GC distributions from step-07 and escape velocities from step-09.","inputs":["V_GC (step-07)","V_esc (step-09)"],"outputs":["P_ub (per model)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":480,"end_line":485,"context":"Definition of unbound probability"}]}],"candidates":[{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-001","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel1","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel1","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":43,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel1"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-002","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel2","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel2","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":44,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel2"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-003","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel3","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel3","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":45,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel3"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-004","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel4","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel4","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":46,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel4"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-005","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel5","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel5","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":47,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel5"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-006","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel6","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel6","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":48,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel6"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-007","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel7","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel7","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/20 message.reasoning_content:
We need to fix the two errors:

1. `method_chain[10].depends_on 'step-17' must reference an earlier step id` — In the current method_chain, step-11 (index 10) depends on step-17 which comes later. The error says "must reference an earlier step id". Indeed, step-11 depends on step-17 (which is later in the chain). We need to adjust. The problem: step-11 (candidate_classification) depends on step-17 (escape_or_bound_assessment) and step-10 (orbit_integration). But step-17 is currently placed after step-11, so it's not earlier. The error indicates that the depends_on must reference only earlier steps (i.e., steps with lower indices in the array). So we need to reorder the method_chain? The instruction says: "never renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids — candidate records already reference them. Append new steps at the END with the next sequential id; never insert between existing steps." So we cannot move existing steps around. We can only add new steps at the end. So we need to fix step-11's depends_on to not include step-17, which doesn't exist earlier. But step-11 requires P_ub from step-17. So step-11 should depend on step-17, but step-17 must be earlier. The only way is to reorder by appending, but we are not allowed to delete or renumber. The existing step-17 is later than step-11. We can't change step-11's dependencies to step-17 earlier. The solution: we need to restructure: step-17 should be before step-11. But step-17 already exists as step-17. If we move it, that would change its id? Renumbering is not allowed. So we cannot change step ids. The only allowed actions: add new steps at the end. So we need to make step-11 depend on a step that is earlier. Possibly we can remove the dependency on step-17 from step-11 and instead note that step-11 uses P_ub from step-17 but that step is later, and perhaps we can incorporate that later. But the schema validation requires that depends_on only references earlier steps. So we have to change step-11 to not depend on step-17, and instead maybe have step-11 depend on step-10 and step-17 later (but step-17 is after step-11). We could add a new step later that performs the classification (as a separate step), but that would change the method chain order? Actually, step-11 is already classification; perhaps we could split: step-11 could remain but not depend on step-17; instead, it could depend on step-10 and a future step that provides P_ub. But that future step would be later. Not allowed. Alternatively, we could reorder the chain by appending a new version of step-11 at the end, and mark the old step-11 as something else? But we cannot delete or renumber. The instruction says we may add or split method steps, but never renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids. So we can create new steps at the end, but we cannot alter existing step-11. So the only way to satisfy the validation is to change step-11's depends_on to not include step-17. That would break its logical dependency. However, we can note in the summary that P_ub is later computed. But the validation error is strict. So we must adjust step-11's depends_on to remove step-17, and then we can note that step-17 (escape_or_bound_assessment) is a later step that provides P_ub, but classification is done after? This is messy.

Alternatively, maybe the error is about step-10 (index 10) depending on step-17? Looking at method_chain: index 10 is step-11? Let's list the method_chain array:

0: step-01
1: step-02
2: step-03
3: step-04
